name: Dai Shogi
author: Abstract Games factory
description: Dai Shogi (大将棋, 'large shogi'): the historical 15x15 Japanese great shogi from the Kamakura period (~1230 AD), the ancestor of Chu Shogi -- 65 pieces a side of 29 types, no drops, the double-moving Lion (double captures, igui capture-in-place, turn passes) with NO trading restrictions, plus eight weak extra pieces (Iron & Stone Generals, Knight, Angry Boar, Cat Sword, Evil Wolf, Violent Ox, Flying Dragon) that all promote to Gold. Win by capturing ALL the opponent's royals (King, plus a Prince if a Drunk Elephant promoted); there is no check rule. Promotion in the far five ranks on entry or on a capture.
